title: iris spuria var. notha
intro: Iris spuria var. notha, also known as the Dalmatian Iris, is a plant species in the iris family that is native to the Balkans and parts of Europe. The plant's roots contain medicinal properties, including iridin and glycosides, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Iris spuria var. notha include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
